diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional syncope in pulmonary hypertension indicates the RV cannot augment output - a high-risk marker of severe disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive exertional dyspnoea over months with resting hypoxaemia (SpO2 92%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Progressive exertional dyspnoea with resting hypoxaemia indicates significant pulmonary vascular/right-heart disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Ankle oedema and abdominal fullness (systemic venous congestion)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral oedema and hepatic congestion/ascites reflect systemic venous congestion from RV failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: No orthopnoea/PND/bibasal crackles; no acute pleuritic chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of orthopnoea/PND/crackles points away from primarily left-sided heart failure.
A chronic progressive course rather than sudden pleuritic onset argues against acute PE.
Fatigue reflects the reduced cardiac output of right-heart failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: RV heave, loud P2, right-sided S3, markedly raised JVP with prominent 'a' wave, tender hepatomegaly

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An RV heave with a loud P2 and raised JVP is the cardinal examination signature of pulmonary hypertension with right ventricular pressure overload/failure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated pulmonary artery pressure and RV dysfunction on echocardiography/right heart catheterisation

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated estimated pulmonary artery pressure with right ventricular hypertrophy/dilatation and dysfunction; right heart catheterisation confirms and characterises pulmonary hypertension
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Pulmonary hypertension and RV function are not characterised and group-specific treatment cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Elevated pulmonary pressures with RV dysfunction confirm pulmonary hypertension.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Pulmonary hypertension with decompensating right ventricular failure
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Exertional presyncope with progressive exertional dyspnoea, resting hypoxaemia and systemic venous congestion, with (sealed) an RV heave/loud P2/raised JVP, indicates high-risk pulmonary hypertension with right heart failure requiring urgent echocardiography/specialist assessment and strict activity restriction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Estimates pulmonary artery pressure and assesses RV function - the key first-line investigation.
Gauge right-heart strain/severity, assess for arrhythmia/RV strain, and guide any diuresis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CT pulmonary angiogram / V-Q scan (for chronic thromboembolic disease)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses for chronic thromboembolic pulmonary hypertension as a treatable cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance about deconditioning with a routine referral and continued activity
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: High-risk pulmonary hypertension with exertional syncope needs urgent assessment and strict rest, not routine follow-up with continued exertion.
